summary: Recent updates to the Web Audio API have included improvements focused on performance optimization. Specifically, the integration of clang-tidy's performance-trivially-destructible pass helps to enhance the efficiency of memory management in audio processing tasks. While users may not see a direct visual change, these optimizations are crucial for ensuring smoother audio experiences in web applications, particularly for features that rely on real-time audio manipulation and playback. **This ensures that web audio applications run more efficiently, reducing lag and improving overall performance.**
